text.title: ES Fundamentals Worked Example
text.summary: Worked example deciding ES vs state-stored for an Order aggregate.

example.title: Order: ES chosen with full audit trail
example.code:
Aggregate: Order
Step 1 — audit need: Regulator (payment processor) requires full history of every order modification. Customer support frequently needs "what did this order look like before refund". → audit_required = true
Step 2 — schema stability: Last 6 months: 2 entity changes (added carrier field; added discount). → schema_stable = true
Step 3 — event catalog: OrderPlaced, ItemAdded, ItemRemoved, DiscountApplied, OrderShipped, OrderCancelled. All past-tense business facts.
Step 4 — stream strategy: stream_id = order UUID. One stream per Order instance.
Step 5 — decision record: Decision: use_event_sourcing. Rationale: regulator + customer-support replay need; schema stable enough. Projections planned: OrdersListReadModel, OrderDetailsReadModel. Snapshot policy: every 100 events (see event-sourcing-snapshots).
example.note: All five rules satisfied; decision is "use_event_sourcing".

example.title: UserProfile: ES rejected (schema thrashes)
example.code:
Aggregate: UserProfile
Step 1 — audit need: Minimal. Profile changes are routine; no regulatory replay required.
Step 2 — schema stability: Last 6 months: 7 breaking changes (preferences refactored 3 times, address split into 4 fields, ...). → schema_stable = false
Step 3-5: STOP per decision-gate. Use state-stored UserProfile with ddd-domain-events for notifications.
